creator: feld@FreeBSD.org
text:
MariaDB has several versions that roughly coordinate with MySQL versions. I believe it looks like this:


MariaDB 5.1 == MySQL 5.1
MariaDB 5.2 and 5.3 == MySQL 5.1 + 5.5 backports
MariaDB 5.5 == MySQL 5.5
MariaDB 10.0 ~= MySQL 5.6 (not quite everything pulled in)
MariaDB 10.1 -- not sure? Between MySQL 5.7 and 6.0?


MariaDB isn't quite as different from MariaDB as one might think. We're talking about feature differences while the core is largely identical. If MySQL has a vulnerability, it's extremely likely it's also in MariaDB.
